data.content:
This is an **Android tombstone** from a .NET MAUI app crash. Let me identify the key details and attempt to symbolicate the Mono runtime frames.

**Crash Summary:**
- **App**: `com.companyname.MauiTest` (.NET MAUI)
- **Signal**: `SIGABRT` — abort message: *"not compatible"*
- **Runtime**: `libmonosgen-2.0.so` (Mono, used by .NET on Android)
- **Architecture**: `arm64`

Frames #01–#07 are in `libmonosgen-2.0.so` (BuildId: `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`). Frame #08 is JIT-compiled managed code. Let me try to fetch debug symbols from the Microsoft symbol server.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-930605d23cca49f6a1d7f6594ba3e698 && \
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be" && \
# Try Microsoft symbol server with ELF build-id format
curl -sS -L -o libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg -w "%{http_code}" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1
arguments.description: Download Mono debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download Mono debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-930605d23cca49f6a1d7f6594ba3e698 && \
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be" && \
# Also try alternative symbol server URL format
curl -sS -L -o libmonosgen-2.0.so.sym -w "%{http_code}" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>&1

data.reasoningText:
This is an Android tombstone/crash log, not an Apple .ips crash log. The apple-crash-symbolication skill is specifically for Apple platforms (iOS, tvOS, Mac Catalyst, macOS), so it won't help here.

Let me analyze this crash:

1. **Platform**: Android (arm64)
2. **App**: com.companyname.MauiTest (a .NET MAUI app)
3. **Signal**: SIGABRT with abort message "not compatible"
4. **Key library**: `libmonosgen-2.0.so` — this is the Mono runtime used by .NET MAUI on Android


5. **Stack frames**: Most of the crash is happening in the Mono runtime (frames #01-#07 with BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`), with frame #08 pointing to JIT-compiled managed code in anonymous memory

To symbolicate these frames, I'd need the debug symbols for that specific build of `libmonosgen-2.0.so`, which can potentially be sourced from the .NET runtime repositories or your build artifacts.

The Microsoft symbol server uses a specific URL format with the BuildId to locate debug symbols for ELF binaries. I can try constructing the proper URL to fetch the symbols, though the exact path depends on how the symbols are published.

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-930605d23cca49f6a1d7f6594ba3e698 && \
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be" && \
# Try Microsoft symbol server with ELF build-id format
curl -sS -L -o libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg -w "%{http_code}" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1
arguments.description: Download Mono debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server

cd /tmp/sv-930605d23cca49f6a1d7f6594ba3e698 && \
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be" && \
# Also try alternative symbol server URL format
curl -sS -L -o libmonosgen-2.0.so.sym -w "%{http_code}" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>&1
